paragraph: The single-family market in Bogota is a real seller's market. Over the last six months, 23 homes sold at a median price of $643,000, with nearly two-thirds selling above asking and a sale-to-list ratio of 1.024. Median days on market of 21 days tells you homes priced right are moving quickly, and multiple offer situations are common. Sellers have leverage here. The range is broad, from $320,000 to $800,000, which means the market rewards precision in pricing. Overprice even slightly and you'll sit. Price competitively and you're likely to see competing offers. If you're buying in Bogota, you're entering a competitive market where the best homes draw real interest fast. If you're selling, condition and pricing are everything, but the data shows that when both are right, you'll get the price you're asking for and likely a bit more.
